Scurrula serangodostemon is native to India and other parts of Asia, and it is known to be a hemiparasitic plant that grows on other plants for nutrients. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in managing conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
